roles.definition: Integration role for adapting one interface, contract, or system shape to another.
Reference role for stable addressing, linking, or anchoring within a registry or index.
Documentation role for review, inspection, or evaluation records.
Security role for authentication, authorization, session identity, or access-control responsibility.
Quality role for performance or comparison measurement artifacts.
Architecture-support role for defining or protecting responsibility, module, or integration boundaries.
Role for structure and assembly of user-visible artifacts or interface units.
Style role for presentation-layer mutations of composed user-visible structure.
Build-system role for build pipeline, compilation, bundling, or build-tool automation responsibility.
Persistence role for cache behavior, retained computed state, or temporary storage policy.
Indexing-registry role for organized reference listings or discoverable item collections.
Architecture-support role for command-line entrypoints or command orchestration.
Integration role for consuming or calling an external or internal service boundary.
Configuration role for structured options, setup policy, or configurable runtime/tooling values.
Architecture-support role for shared contracts, interfaces, schemas, or behavioral agreements.
Security role for cryptographic behavior, key handling, hashing, signing, or encryption responsibility.
Operations-support role for deployment behavior, release movement, or environment rollout responsibility.
Data-model role for transfer-shaped output or structured exchange payloads.
Surface-system role for entrypoints into a runtime, UI, app, or execution surface.
Configuration role for environment variables, environment-specific setup, or runtime environment contracts.
Quality-support role for stable test or validation data.
Integration role for mediating access to a system, service, or boundary.
Documentation role for status, health, or readiness review artifacts.
Surface-system role for hosting, mounting, or containing a runtime/UI/system surface.
Localization role for internationalization structures, translation wiring, or language-aware runtime support.
Indexing-registry role for stable identifiers.
Indexing-registry role for lookup or discovery structures.
Operations-support role for infrastructure shape, infrastructure policy, or environment resource structure.

Documentation role for enumerating existing artifacts, migration state, classification status, or cleanup targets without itself defining policy, formal specification, workflow, or execution behavior.
Role for static reference meaning, domain reference content, or declarative support data.
Localization role for locale-specific messages, formatting, translation payloads, or audience-localized data.
Role for behavior, state, decisions, and response.
Observability role for emitted logs, logging behavior, or diagnostic event output.
Indexing-registry role for declared inventory, package, or structural listing metadata.
Integration role for transforming one data, domain, or interface shape into another.
Migration role for transition, conversion, version-shift, or one-time state/schema movement work.
Quality-support role for simulated behavior or replacement test doubles.
Operations or observability role for monitoring signals, health visibility, or operational watch behavior.
Documentation role for bounded explanatory or implementation notes that clarify context without defining policy, formal specification, workflow, audit findings, or planned work.
Build-system role for package assembly, distribution shape, bundling output, or publishable artifact structure.
Performance role for performance-sensitive behavior, measurement output, or optimization evidence.
Documentation role for intended work, sequencing, or implementation direction.
Documentation role for governing rules, constraints, or decision policy.
Integration role for supplying a bounded capability, dependency, service, or value source to a consumer context.
Indexing-registry role for canonical registered data.
Integration role for resolving references, lookups, routing decisions, or context mappings.
Role for output shaping and consumer-ready result modeling.
Style role for presentation-layer mutations of user-visible result output.
Surface-system role for route, path, or dispatch behavior.
Data-model role for structural validation, shape definition, or data contract modeling.
Security role for secret references, secret access policy, credential handling, or protected configuration.
Configuration role for user, system, runtime, or tool settings that tune behavior without owning behavior itself.
Documentation role for formal specification.
Persistence role for stored state, storage access, persistence contracts, or retained data behavior.
Observability role for emitted measurements, traces, metrics, or runtime visibility payloads.
Quality-support role for executable or documented verification.
Indexing-registry role for reusable lexical, design, semantic, or symbolic tokens.
Networking role for transport behavior, protocol exchange, message movement, or communication boundary mechanics.
Data-model role for type definitions.
Historical role from pre-current concern split.
Architecture-support role for connecting modules, dependencies, or system parts.
Concurrency role for asynchronous, parallel, queued, or background execution behavior.
Documentation role for process, sequence, or operating procedure.
